title: TDD prompt followed: thorough test-first coverage present
evidence: 5 *.test.ts files with 66 tests (src/queries.test.ts:19, src/normalizer.test.ts:15, src/tools.test.ts:13); test_coverage=1.0 from scores.json
suggestion: None — TDD prompt instruction satisfied with broad unit coverage of normalizer, loader, queries, players, and tools.

title: Player nationality and position filters use exact equality, not partial/normalized match
evidence: src/players.ts:27 `p.nationality.toLowerCase() === q` and src/players.ts:37 position `=== q`; club/name use includes() but nationality/position require an exact string
suggestion: Accept partial/case-insensitive nationality (e.g. 'brazilian' vs 'Brazil') or document the exact-value contract; minor since the spec examples ('Brazil') match exactly.

title: Statistical analysis exceeds the single-aggregate minimum
evidence: src/queries.ts:242 getStatistics supports biggest_wins, avg_goals, home_win_rate, and top_scorers
suggestion: None — R10 requires at least one aggregate; four are provided.

title: Competition coverage spans all three named competitions plus extended sources
evidence: src/loader.ts:237,251,265 tag Brasileirão / Copa do Brasil / Copa Libertadores, with dedup and source-priority merge of the extended dataset (src/loader.ts:292)
suggestion: None — R5 satisfied; merge logic avoids double-counting across overlapping CSVs.

title: Cup match round is parsed as a string but assigned to a numeric NormalizedMatch.round
evidence: src/loader.ts:116 CupMatch.round = row['round'] (string) then src/loader.ts:253 add({... round: m.round ...}) into NormalizedMatch whose round is numeric elsewhere; build still passes (test_coverage=1.0)
suggestion: Coerce cup round via toInt() or widen NormalizedMatch.round to string|number for consistency; cosmetic, does not affect query results.
